tool.name: SOPA Report
tool.command:
@CMD_UNZIP_ZARR@
## Determine the SpatialData directory name SDATA_DIR=\$(find . -maxdepth 1 -name "*.zarr" -type d | head -n 1) &&
## Run report generation sopa report "\${SDATA_DIR}" report.html &&
## Move report to expected output location mv report.html '$output_report'
tool.help:
**What it does**
SOPA Report generates an interactive HTML quality control report for spatial omics data. The report provides comprehensive visualizations and statistics to assess data quality and processing results.
**Requirements**
- Input should be a processed SpatialData object (ideally after aggregation) - Works best with aggregated data but can generate reports at any pipeline stage
**Report Contents**
The HTML report includes multiple sections with figures and quality metrics:
**1. Data Overview** - Sample metadata and processing parameters - Technology platform information - Dataset dimensions (number of cells, genes, channels)
**2. Cell Statistics** - Total number of detected cells - Cell size distribution (area in µm²) - Transcript count distribution (if transcript data present) - Channel intensity distributions (if fluorescence data present)
**3. Spatial Visualizations** - Cell boundary overlays on tissue images - Spatial distribution of cell types (if annotated) - Transcript density maps (if transcript data present) - Channel intensity spatial plots
**4. Gene/Channel Expression** - Top expressed genes (if transcript data present) - Gene expression distributions - Channel intensity heatmaps - Marker expression patterns
**5. Quality Control Metrics** - Segmentation quality indicators - Coverage statistics (% tissue segmented) - Cell density across tissue - Background signal levels
**6. Cell-Type Composition** (if annotated) - Cell-type abundance pie charts - Cell-type proportions table - Spatial distribution of cell types - Cell-type co-localization patterns
**7. Spatial Metrics** (optional) - Spatial autocorrelation statistics - Neighborhood analysis - Cell-cell interaction metrics - Tissue organization patterns
**Use Cases**
The report is useful for: - **Quality assessment**: Verify segmentation and data quality - **Parameter optimization**: Compare different processing parameters - **Data exploration**: Initial exploration before detailed analysis - **Documentation**: Permanent record of processing results - **Sharing results**: Share with collaborators or include in supplementary materials
**Interactive Features**
The HTML report is interactive and includes: - Zoomable plots - Hoverable data points for detailed information - Collapsible sections for easy navigation - Exportable figures (right-click on plots)
**Report Interpretation**
**Good quality indicators**: - Uniform cell density across tissue - Clear cell boundaries in overlays - Transcript counts follow expected distributions - Low background signal - Cell sizes match expected biology
**Potential issues to watch for**: - Very high or low cell counts (segmentation parameters may need adjustment) - Patchy cell coverage (tissue quality or segmentation issues) - Bimodal transcript distributions (technical artifacts) - High background in specific regions (imaging issues) - Abnormal cell size distributions (segmentation artifacts)
**Performance Notes**
- Report generation is fast (typically <1 minute) - Large datasets may produce large HTML files (10-50 MB) - All figures are embedded in the HTML (self-contained)
**Output**
- Single HTML file containing all visualizations and metrics - Can be viewed in any modern web browser - No external dependencies required - Can be shared easily via email or web
**More Information**
See the `SOPA report documentation <https://prism-oncology.github.io/sopa/tutorials/cli_usage/#pipeline-report>`_.
